title: campanula punctata subsp. hondoensis
intro: Campanula punctata subsp. hondoensis, also known as Hondo bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusement, and it can also be used in the form of a tincture or salve for topical application.
